m	Z	
Implementation of Harwell-Boeing read/write.
At the moment not the full Harwell-Boeing format is supported. Supported
features are:
    - assembled, non-symmetric, real matrices
    - integer for pointer/indices
    - exponential format for float values, and int format
csc_matrix)

	IntFormat
	ExpFormat)

Default title
Create a HBInfo instance from an existing sparse matrix.
        Parameters
        ----------
        m : sparse matrix
            the HBInfo instance will derive its parameters from m
        title : str
            Title to put in the HB header
        key : str
            Key
        mxtype : HBMatrixType
            type of the input matrix
        fmt : dict
            not implemented
        Returns
        -------
        hb_info : HBInfo instance
        F

Create a HBInfo instance from a file object containing a matrix in the
        HB format.
        Parameters
        ----------
        fid : file-like matrix
            File or file-like object containing a matrix in the HB format.
        Returns
        -------
        hb_info : HBInfo instance

propertyr/
Create a HBFile instance.
        Parameters
        ----------
        file : file-object
            StringIO work as well
        hb_info : HBInfo, optional
            Should be given as an argument for writing, in which case the file
            should be writable.
        N)
_fidr	
_hb_info)
file
hb_infor
HBFile.__init__c
HBFile.titlec
HBFile.keyc
type
HBFile.typec
HBFile.structurec
HBFile.storagec
read_matrix
HBFile.read_matrixc
write_matrix
HBFile.write_matrix)
Read HB-format file.
    Parameters
    ----------
    path_or_open_file : path-like or file-like
        If a file-like object, it is used as-is. Otherwise, it is opened
        before reading.
    Returns
    -------
    data : scipy.sparse.csc_matrix instance
        The data read from the HB file as a sparse matrix.
    Notes
    -----
    At the moment not the full Harwell-Boeing format is supported. Supported
    features are:
        - assembled, non-symmetric, real matrices
        - integer for pointer/indices
        - exponential format for float values, and int format
    Examples
    --------
    We can read and write a harwell-boeing format file:
    >>> from scipy.io.harwell_boeing import hb_read, hb_write
    >>> from scipy.sparse import csr_matrix, eye
    >>> data = csr_matrix(eye(3))  # create a sparse matrix
    >>> hb_write("data.hb", data)  # write a hb file
    >>> print(hb_read("data.hb"))  # read a hb file
      (0, 0)	1.0
      (1, 1)	1.0
      (2, 2)	1.0
    c
_get_matrix
hb_read.<locals>._get_matrixrv
hasattr
open)
path_or_open_filer
Write HB-format file.
    Parameters
    ----------
    path_or_open_file : path-like or file-like
        If a file-like object, it is used as-is. Otherwise, it is opened
        before writing.
    m : sparse-matrix
        the sparse matrix to write
    hb_info : HBInfo
        contains the meta-data for write
    Returns
    -------
    None
    Notes
    -----
    At the moment not the full Harwell-Boeing format is supported. Supported
    features are:
        - assembled, non-symmetric, real matrices
        - integer for pointer/indices
        - exponential format for float values, and int format
    Examples
    --------
    We can read and write a harwell-boeing format file:
    >>> from scipy.io.harwell_boeing import hb_read, hb_write
    >>> from scipy.sparse import csr_matrix, eye
    >>> data = csr_matrix(eye(3))  # create a sparse matrix
    >>> hb_write("data.hb", data)  # write a hb file
    >>> print(hb_read("data.hb"))  # read a hb file
      (0, 0)	1.0
      (1, 1)	1.0
      (2, 2)	1.0
    Fr
_set_matrix3
hb_write.<locals>._set_matrixry
